channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: CAS SciFinder Upcoming Webinars
item.description: RPI Libraries invites you to attend upcoming vendor-hosted CAS SciFinder webinars that include topics like Agentic AI in CAS SciFinder, How to search structures and retrieve references in CAS IP Finder™, and CAS Formulations in CAS IP Finder™ Explore on-demand webinars, including: What’s New in CAS SciFinder®?, 2026 Trends to Watch: Expert Panel, and Data Mining and Analysis: Unlocking New Research Perspectives with AI See the full announcement for registration information.
item.author: Students

item.title: Graduate Student Lunch and Learn - Preparing for the academic/industry job market. Registration Required.
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 10:30 AM - 12:30 PM - Join us for faculty and staff to help you prepare for the academic/industry job market with their firsthand experience. Registration Required. RSVP to sanchn2@rpi.edu
item.author: Students

item.title: Tech Talk and Pizza with Micron Technology
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 9, 2026 4:00 PM - 6:00 PM - RPI welcomes Shyam Surthi, Distinguished Member of Technical Staff at Micron Technology, for an in-person student session. He will share insights on advanced NAND technology, the evolving semiconductor landscape, and career opportunities at Micron. Join to learn from an industry expert, ask questions, and connect with peers. Pizza will be provided.
item.author: Students

item.title: Exponent - Career Opportunities in Engineering & Scientific Consulting (Tuesday, April 7th 2026, 6:00 pm - 7:00 pm EDT)
item.description: When: Tuesday, April 7th 2026, 6:00 pm - 7:00 pm EDT Location: Darrin Communications Center, Center for Career and Professional Development -DCC 209, Troy, New York, United States
item.author: Students

item.title: Faculty/Staff Memorial Service to be Held April 14
item.description: In remembrance of the individuals we lost this past year, the Pillars of Rensselaer invite all members of the Rensselaer community, including alumni and friends, to attend this year’s Faculty and Staff Memorial Service Faculty/Staff Memorial Service.
item.author: Community

item.title: Designing Sustainability: From Entrepreneurialism to Environmental Justice
item.description: Wednesday, April 8 | 4-6pm | Student Union Theater (1st floor) Come to this special panel event featuring guest speakers Alexis Goldsmith (Beyond Plastics), and RPI alumni Gavin McIntyre (Ecovative CEO), and Bobbi Wilding (Clean + Healthy Executive Director). Learn about their innovative solutions to sustainability and have a Q&A afterwards. Stay for a fun activity and food!
item.author: Community

item.title: Constellations
item.description: Thursday / April 9, 2026 at 5:00 pm Featuring guest artist Nick Dunston With DJ Rekha and Joey Latka Directed by Michael Century and Nick Dunston Presented by Arts Department Performance A space performance by RPI Contemporary Improvisation Ensemble
item.author: Community

item.title: Center for Research Advancing Financial Technologies (CRAFT) Annual Conference
item.description: The Center for Research toward Advancing Financial Technologies (CRAFT) invites you to its annual conference meeting on Thursday, April 30, 8:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m. in the CBIS Auditorium. Registration is required; business attire is expected. CRAFT is an NSF-funded industry-university research center. The event features keynote talks and cutting-edge fintech research and practice presentations, hosted by RPI and Stevens Institute of Technology.
item.author: Community

item.title: Math - Class of ’27 Lecture Series | April 7 & April 8
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - The Class of ’27 Lecture Series, established in 1960 to honor the first chair of the Math Sciences Department, holds two special lectures annually. Lecture I, on April 7, will present “Nonlocal PDEs and Quantum Optics”. Lecture II, on April 8, features “Inverse Problems in Quantum Optics”, by speaker John C. Schotland from Yale University.
item.author: Community

item.title: NEW at CCPD: The Career Closet Open House!
item.description: Come check out the NEW CCPD Career Closet and see how we’re helping students show up with confidence! 🗓 Thursday, April 9 | ⏰ 11 a.m.–3 p.m. 📍 DCC, Great Hall 👗 Take a tour of the Career Closet (DCC 121) and learn about the new “My Career Closet” program. 🎁 BONUS: The f̳i̳r̳s̳t̳ ̳5̳0̳ ̳s̳t̳u̳d̳e̳n̳t̳s̳ to use the digital My Career Closet receive a FREE voucher! Don’t miss out on this exciting new CCPD resource—come see what the Career Closet can do for you!
item.author: Community

item.title: Graduate Students Needed to Solve National Security Problems
item.description: Earn credits this fall as you join teams to solve real problems presented by the Department of Defense. Students from all schools, including MBA students, are encouraged to register the fall Hacking 4 Defense course. Reach out to Prof. Clint Ballinger at ballic2@rpi.edu for more information. All disciplines are welcome and encouraged to register.
item.author: Community

item.title: Intellectual Property & Technology Licensing Seminar
item.description: Are you working on groundbreaking research? Curious about how to protect, commercialize, or launch a start-up based on your innovations through IP? Join us on April 14th for an exciting campuswide seminar hosted by the Office of Intellectual Property and Technology Licensing (IPTL) at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI)! This seminar is your chance to learn how IPTL supports faculty and students in translating research into real-world impact through patents, licensing, or industry…
item.author: Community

item.title: Writing for Fellowships & Grants: Workshop for PhD Students
item.description: Learn approaches to presenting content & editing principles to develop concise effective applications. Previous participants have secured major awards, including international fellowships! April 8th, 2:00-4:00 PM, In person. Register to attend! https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_6L3FFwv0qCROrvE Questions? madige@rpi.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: Dr. Marlene Belfort: “Navigating Scientific Challenges”
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 9, 2026 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM - Prof. Marlene Belfort, born in Cape Town to German-Jewish refugees, studied biochemistry and microbiology at the University of Cape Town, earned her PhD at UC Irvine, and completed postdoctoral work in Jerusalem and at Northwestern. A member of the U.S. National Academy of Sciences and an award-winning scientist, she is now a Distinguished Professor at the University at Albany, SUNY, known for research on introns and inteins and for advocating…
item.author: Community

item.title: Transfer Student Slushie Social
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 9, 2026 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM - This is a chance for Transfer Students, Staff and Faculty who work with transfer students to get together in a fun atmosphere and get to know one another better. Slushies and other snacks will be provided. Come join the fun!
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Students Experiment with Music Performance for April 9 Concert
item.description: RPI students will experiment with music performance in “Constellations” at EMPAC on April 9. The performance explores structured improvisation, and will be led by sound artist Nick Dunston. Also featured will be a generative algorithm in place of a traditional score, as well as an appearance by DJ Rekha. Learn more: https://bit.ly/4c1gLEW
item.author: Community

item.title: MSE Distinguished Lecture: Dr. David Muller
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 9, 2026 12:30 PM - 1:30 PM - “Imaging the Properties of Atoms and Fields down to the Picometer Scale inside Materials and Devices” Dr. David Muller Cornell
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Security - How to Recognize and Report Business Email Compromise (BEC) Emails
item.description: Business Email Compromise (BEC) is a sophisticated cybercrime targeting Organizations and Universities where attackers impersonate trusted individuals including Executives, Faculty or Staff, and trusted vendors. Through impersonation, these bad actors will attempt to trick victims into transferring funds, revealing sensitive data, and other social engineering tactics attempting to extract personally identifiable information (PII).
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Supervisors: Performance and Coaching
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 9, 2026 8:00 AM - 10:00 AM - As a manager, you play a central role in guiding the performance process for your employees—working collaboratively to align expectations, support development, and monitor progress toward achieving goals. This workshop introduces the core principles of performance coaching, including how to identify and evaluate performance gaps, set effective goals, and lead the RPI MAX performance process. Participants will also explore practical tools for…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Staff: Leveraging Feedback and Career Opportunities
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 9, 2026 10:00 AM - 11:30 AM - As an employee, you work in partnership with your manager to align expectations and set meaningful goals. Throughout the year, you check in together, recalibrate as needed, and celebrate progress and achievements. This workshop focuses on supporting your individual career development, preparing for the annual manager evaluation, setting effective goals, and using tools that can help you advance your career.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Teaching and Learning Collaboratory 2026 Spring In-Person RPI Faculty and Graduate TA’s Workshop: Teaching and Mentoring with Neurodivergence in Mind
item.description:
In this session, faculty are introduced to a greater understanding of neurodivergence in college students. The program clarifies what neurodivergence is, what it isn’t, and offers practical tools for faculty that support structured, high-expectation learning environments where neurodivergent students can succeed. We’ll focus on strategies and concepts that support the maintenance of academic rigor while reducing unnecessary barriers to learning.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
